prompt: Create a communication plan for a 6-month ERP migration project with 4 stakeholder groups: C-suite sponsors, department heads, end users (200+), and the external vendor team.
assertions: Output segments stakeholders into at least 4 distinct communication groups with differentiated needs
Output specifies at least 3 different communication channels mapped to stakeholder segments
Output includes a cadence or frequency recommendation per stakeholder group
Output describes a feedback mechanism or measurement approach for communication effectiveness

prompt: A project sponsor complains they receive too many operational emails and miss strategic updates. Redesign the communication approach for executive stakeholders.
assertions: Output distinguishes between strategic and operational communication and reduces noise for executives
Output recommends a specific cadence and channel optimized for executive consumption patterns
Output includes an escalation path for critical items that require immediate executive attention
Output proposes a feedback loop to validate the redesigned approach is working

prompt: Build a communication plan for a cross-functional team spanning 3 time zones (US-East, EU-Central, Asia-Pacific) working on a platform modernization initiative.
assertions: Output addresses time zone overlap windows and recommends synchronous vs asynchronous channels accordingly
Output segments communication needs by geographic region or time zone cluster
Output defines a cadence that accounts for regional working hours and avoids off-hours overload
Output includes a channel strategy with at least one asynchronous and one synchronous option

prompt: Evaluate this existing communication plan and identify gaps: 'Weekly status email to all stakeholders every Monday. Monthly steering committee meeting. Ad-hoc Slack messages for urgent issues.'
assertions: Output identifies the lack of stakeholder segmentation as a gap
Output flags the absence of a structured feedback or effectiveness measurement mechanism
Output recommends differentiated cadence or channels for different stakeholder groups
Output suggests adding trigger-based or event-driven communications beyond the fixed schedule
